title: tetramolopium humile
intro: Tetramolopium humile, also known as creeping nebeda, is a low-growing, spreading plant native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as having antiseptic and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, Tetramolopium humile can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
